title: In San Francisco, Some Home Sellers Now Ask for OpenAI or Anthropic Stock
summary: Even before OpenAI and Anthropic go public, they are distorting home sales in the San Francisco Bay Area, as people race to buy and sellers ask for stock instead of cash.

title: Meta Unveils an A.I. Image Generator
summary: Muse Image, which can create realistic images for users on Instagram and WhatsApp, is the company’s latest attempt to catch up in the global artificial intelligence race.

title: Businesses Fear Politicization as Trump Gains More Power Over U.S. Agencies
summary: A Supreme Court ruling that presidents can fire independent regulators without cause has added volatility for industries that prefer stable enforcement.

title: To Reduce Electrical Grid Strain Amid Heat Wave, Data Centers Are Ordered to Use Backup Power
summary: As triple-digit temperatures engulf much of the United States, the Trump administration wants grid managers to require the use of backup power that often goes unused.

title: Alibaba’s A.I. Is a Hit, but Hard to Turn Into a Moneymaker
summary: The Chinese company’s models have won over developers worldwide, but they are open source — so they can be used and modified freely.

title: Why A.I. Distillation Has Become a Hot Topic in the Race with China
summary: U.S. companies complain that competitors in China are unfairly copying their A.I. systems using a technique that has been around for years.

title: Microsoft Lays Off Thousands of Xbox Employees, Closes Game Studios
summary: The layoffs were part of wider cuts at Microsoft, as the company prioritizes spending on artificial intelligence.

title: How Meta’s Threads Became as Popular as X
summary: The social platform that Meta once positioned as a rival to Elon Musk’s X now has 500 million monthly users. It increasingly resembles Reddit.

title: In San Francisco’s A.I. Era, Even $180,000 Tech Salaries Are No Longer Enough
summary: As OpenAI and Anthropic prepare to go public, tech workers making six figures are grousing that they cannot compete with the new A.I. elite. Some doubt they can afford to stay.

title: Fable Ban Reversed + Dr. Dana Suskind on Parenting With A.I. + Prediction Market Drama
summary: What we learned from the government’s biggest attempt yet to control who can gain access to the most powerful new A.I. models.

title: Microsoft Disclosure Provides Rare Glimpse of Tax Haven Tactics
summary: Other U.S. companies will soon need to provide similar reports as a new European directive takes effect.

title: Tesla Driver Using Autopilot in Texas Crash Is Charged With Manslaughter
summary: A front-door video camera of the crash, which killed a woman inside her home, showed the Tesla plowing into a house through its driveway.

title: Tesla Sales Surge 25% on Recovery in Europe
summary: Price cuts helped the electric automaker rebound in Europe in the second quarter, offsetting declines in the United States.

title: Is a ‘Frictionless’ Society a Trap?
summary: Eliminating whatever stands between you and a task sounds nice, but it probably has more to offer companies than users.

title: The Yoto Music Box Is a Ray of Hope Amid the ‘Techlash’
summary: Amid widespread “techlash” over addictive screens and apps, the Yoto, an audio player for children, shows there’s still a way to make money while doing something nice.

title: U.S. Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Most Powerful A.I. Models
summary: The move allows Anthropic to bring its most powerful technologies back online, de-escalating a feud with the Trump administration.

title: BMW Will Build a New Electric S.U.V. in South Carolina
summary: The German company is doubling down on electric vehicles even as other automakers pull back after acknowledging billions of dollars in losses.

title: Bending Spoons, Owner of AOL and Other Old Internet Brands, Is Going Public
summary: Bending Spoons, an Italian company that buys aging internet companies, is going public this week at a potential value of $19 billion.

title: How A.I. Is Changing the Way Politicians Run for Office
summary: A.I.-generated images are the public face of this election overhaul. Behind the scenes, campaigns are using the technology to analyze voter data, craft campaign materials and write custom messages.

title: Many Child Safety Features on Social Apps Don’t Work, Report Finds
summary: Researchers found that teens can discover harmful content, connect with unknown adults and easily bypass time limits.
